title: Oscar and the Missing Magnets
synopsis: When all the magnets disappear from Oscar's science class, he must use his detective skills and scientific knowledge to solve the mystery. With help from his friends Maya and Sam, Oscar discovers that sometimes the answer is right above your head!

pages.text: Oscar loved science class. He loved mixing colors. He loved growing plants. But most of all, he loved magnets! Today, Mrs. Chen had a big surprise. "We're going to learn about magnets!" she said. Oscar's dark eyes sparkled with excitement. He bounced in his chair.

illustration.description: Oscar, a cheerful boy with dark curly hair and big brown eyes, sits at a colorful classroom desk with his hand raised excitedly. Mrs. Chen, an Asian teacher with kind eyes and a lab coat, stands at the front holding a large red horseshoe magnet. The classroom is bright and cheerful with science posters on the walls.
illustration.alt: Oscar sits excitedly in science class as Mrs. Chen holds up a red magnet

pages.text: Mrs. Chen opened the magnet box. Her smile turned into a frown. "Oh my!" she said. "The magnets are gone!" Oscar looked inside the box. It was completely empty. Maya, his friend with curly red hair, gasped. "Where did they go?" asked Sam, who sat behind Oscar.

illustration.description: Mrs. Chen looks puzzled as she holds an empty wooden box, peering inside with a confused expression. Oscar, Maya (a girl with bright red curly hair and freckles), and Sam (a boy with short blonde hair) all lean forward to look at the empty box. The classroom setting has warm yellows and blues.
illustration.alt: Mrs. Chen and three students look into an empty box where magnets should be

pages.text: "This is a mystery!" said Oscar. He put on his thinking face. Oscar loved mysteries almost as much as he loved science. "Don't worry, Mrs. Chen," he said. "Detective Oscar is on the case!" Maya giggled. Sam clapped his hands. Mrs. Chen smiled. "Good luck, Detective Oscar!"

illustration.description: Oscar strikes a detective pose with his finger to his chin and a determined expression. He wears a bright blue shirt and his dark hair catches the light. Maya and Sam smile encouragingly behind him, while Mrs. Chen watches with an amused expression. The mood is playful and adventurous.
illustration.alt: Oscar poses like a detective while his friends and teacher watch supportively

pages.text: Oscar walked around the classroom. He looked under desks. He looked in the art supplies. He looked in the reading corner. No magnets anywhere! "Hmm," said Oscar. "Magnets stick to metal things. What metal things are in our classroom?" Maya pointed to the filing cabinet. "That's metal!"

illustration.description: Oscar crouches down examining under a colorful classroom desk, holding a magnifying glass. The classroom is vibrant with rainbow-colored desks, a cozy reading corner with soft pillows, and art supplies scattered on shelves. Maya points toward a gray filing cabinet in the background.
illustration.alt: Oscar searches the classroom with a magnifying glass while Maya points to a metal filing cabinet

pages.text: The three friends checked the filing cabinet. No magnets there. They checked the metal legs of the tables. Nothing! "What about the door?" asked Sam. The door handle was metal too. Oscar looked carefully. Still no magnets! "This is puzzling," said Oscar. "Where could they be?"

illustration.description: The three friends work together as a team, with Oscar examining a door handle, Maya checking table legs, and Sam looking at the filing cabinet. Their expressions show concentration and determination. The scene has warm oranges and greens, creating a cozy investigative atmosphere.
illustration.alt: Oscar, Maya, and Sam search different metal objects in the classroom

pages.text: Just then, Oscar heard a strange sound. Clank! Clank! It came from above. Oscar looked up at the ceiling. There were metal air vents up there! "The sound is coming from up there!" said Oscar. He pointed to the ceiling. Maya and Sam looked up too. "But how do we check up there?" asked Maya.

illustration.description: All three children look up toward the ceiling with wide, curious eyes and open mouths expressing wonder. The ceiling shows metal air vents with a subtle shimmer. Oscar points upward with excitement while Maya and Sam crane their necks. The lighting creates a sense of discovery and mystery.
illustration.alt: Three children look up at the ceiling where metal air vents are visible

pages.text: Mrs. Chen brought a step ladder. She climbed up carefully. "Oh my goodness!" she laughed. "Look at this!" All the magnets were stuck to the air vent! Red ones, blue ones, and horseshoe magnets too. They were all hanging from the metal vent like colorful decorations.

illustration.description: Mrs. Chen stands on a yellow step ladder reaching toward the ceiling vent where colorful magnets (red, blue, silver horseshoe-shaped) are attached in a cluster. Her expression shows delighted surprise. The magnets create a rainbow effect against the white ceiling, and warm light illuminates the scene.
illustration.alt: Mrs. Chen on a ladder discovers all the colorful magnets stuck to a ceiling vent

pages.text: "But how did they get up there?" asked Sam. Oscar smiled his biggest smile. "I know!" he said. "Someone must have thrown them up there. The magnets flew up and stuck to the metal vent!" "That's exactly right!" said Mrs. Chen. "Yesterday, I was showing the other class. One magnet flew too high!"

illustration.description: Oscar has a triumphant expression with his arms raised in victory, his face beaming with pride. Maya and Sam look impressed and happy. Mrs. Chen nods approvingly from the ladder. The scene is filled with warm, celebratory colors - golds and bright blues - showing the joy of solving the mystery.
illustration.alt: Oscar celebrates solving the mystery while his friends and teacher look impressed

pages.text: Mrs. Chen carefully took down all the magnets. "Great detective work, Oscar!" she said. "You used science to solve the mystery!" The friends played with the magnets all morning. They made them stick to paper clips. They made them push each other away. "Science is the best!" said Oscar. And everyone agreed.

illustration.description: All the children sit around a table covered with various colorful magnets, paper clips, and small metal objects. Their faces show joy and wonder as they experiment. Oscar holds two magnets that are pushing apart, Maya watches a magnet pick up paper clips, and Sam examines a horseshoe magnet. The scene is bright and cheerful with rainbow colors.
illustration.alt: Oscar, Maya, and Sam happily experiment with magnets at their classroom table

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: What was missing from Mrs. Chen's classroom?
questions.explanation: The magnets were missing from the magnet box, which started the whole mystery!

questions.question: Where did Oscar find the magnets?
questions.explanation: The magnets were stuck to the metal air vent on the ceiling because magnets are attracted to metal.

questions.question: Who helped Oscar solve the mystery?
questions.explanation: Maya and Sam were Oscar's friends who helped him search the classroom and solve the mystery together.

questions.question: What sound did Oscar hear that gave him a clue?
questions.explanation: Oscar heard 'clank, clank' coming from above, which made him look up at the metal air vents.

questions.question: How did the magnets get on the ceiling?
choices: They flew up and stuck to the metal vent
questions.explanation: Mrs. Chen explained that during yesterday's class, a magnet flew too high and stuck to the metal vent, and the others followed.

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words - Chapter 1
pages.instructions: Choose the correct word to complete each sentence from the story.

sentences.text: Oscar loved _____ class most of all.
Mrs. Chen opened the magnet _____ and found it empty.
Oscar said 'Detective Oscar is on the _____!'

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words - Chapter 2 & 3
pages.instructions: Choose the correct word to complete each sentence from the story.

sentences.text: Oscar heard a strange _____ from above: Clank! Clank!
All the magnets were stuck to the metal air _____.
Oscar used _____ to solve the mystery!
